mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or positive forward returns occur in fewer than 50% of rate-cut months.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 5.0 percent signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending expectations and triggers equity sell-offs within the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in over 60% of months where UNRATE exceeds 5.0, or the signal shows zero predictive power in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a synchronized collapse in consumer and business demand, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: The joint decline produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a late-cycle expansion reflects robust manufacturing output that supports corporate earnings revisions and generates positive short-term equity momentum.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns fail to materialize in over 50% of rising INDPRO observations, or the mechanism reverses direction during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rates above 4.5 percent impose financing constraints on highly leveraged firms and compress valuation multiples, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in more than 65% of high-rate months, or the threshold effect disappears when controlling for inflation expectations.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between falling nonfarm payrolls and rising unemployment confirms structural labor market weakness that depresses earnings forecasts and drives negative quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The combined labor weakness signal yields positive 60-day returns in over 50% of instances, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in real gross domestic product reflect expanding economic activity that directly lifts corporate revenues and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling windows, or positive GDP growth correlates with negative equity returns in out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below 110 points indicates a severe supply-side contraction that triggers immediate risk-off positioning and negative short-term market returns.
failure_condition: The threshold breach fails to predict negative 5-day returns in more than 55% of cases, or the signal loses predictive power after adjusting for seasonal factors.
